description:
This Berry cures the Burned affliction. It works for humans and Pokémon alike. A Pokémon holding it can consume it as a Free Action during its turn. A trainer can consume it during their turn as an action (Use Item). It can be consumed during your turn even while Asleep, Paralyzed, or affected by any combination of afflictions, as long as you are holding it and do not need to dig through anything to get it. The same applies to a Pokémon holding the berry.

This Berry is Bitter.

name: Leaf Stone

description: Allows a Pokémon to evolve when its species would normally require a special condition (e.g. stone, trade, or location). Consumed when used.
name: Evolution Item

description: An item that allows certain Pokémon to evolve.
name: Dusk Stone

description: An item that allows certain Pokémon to evolve.
name: Moon Stone

description: An item that allows certain Pokémon to evolve.
name: Water Stone

description: An item that allows certain Pokémon to evolve.
name: Fire Stone

description: When you appeal to a judge you may use the Bright Charm to raise the judge’s voltage +1. The Bright Charm cannot be used this way again unless repaired with the Bright Charm feature. A Pokémon can only benefit from one accessory at a time.
name: Bright Charm

description: 3/day per day, during any appeal of a contest, add 1d4. If the result of that d4 is a 3 or 4, the Accessory cannot be anymore during this contest. A Pokémon can only benefit from one accessory at a time.
name: Flawless Accessory

description: Once per day, during the introduction appeal of a contest, add 1d4. If the result of that d4 is a 3 or 4, the Accessory cannot be used this way again unless repaired with the Accessorize feature. A Pokémon can only benefit from one accessory at a time.
name: Accessory

description: An item that stuns a target when the wearer naturally rolls 18-20 on an accuracy roll. This item works once a day.
name: Kings Rock

description: An item that deals an additional 4 damage for any attack that matches the plate's type. These plates appear to be related to Arceus and come in many varieties based on type.
name: Elemental Plate

description: An item that increases SPATK by 2 on the holder's first turn in combat. This effect cannot be stacked with stat passives.
name: Wise Glasses

description: An item that increases SPD by 1 for 10 minutes when a Pokémon's ATK or SPATK is lowered by an opponent's effects.
name: Adrenaline Orb

description: If you hit with a super effective or extremely effective attack, deal an additional +4 damage
name: Expert Belt

description: An item that deals an additional 1d6 damage on a damaging attack. The holder takes 2d6 damage when this item activates.
name: Life Orb

description: An item that grans an additional 1d6 HP when using moves that heal the user based on how much damage they were able to deal.
name: Big Root

description: An item that deals 1d4 damage to an opponent that attacks the wearer at melee range.
name: Rocky Helmet

description: An item that clears away all temporary stat reductions. This item is destroyed whe consumed.
name: White Herb

description: An item that increases DEF by 1 for 10 minutes when the holder enters the appropriate terrain. This effect cannot be stacked, and the item is destroyed when activated.
name: Terrain Seeds

description: An item that increases SPDEF by 1, but prevents the wearer from using non-damaging moves. This item does not stack with stat passives.
name: Assault Vest

description: An item that grants the wearer immunity to the damage and effects of weather and powder moves.
name: Safety Goggles

description: An item that grants the wearer immunity to the damage and effects of hazards, once per combat encounter.
name: Heavy Duty Boots

description: An item that allows created, matching weather to last for 5 minutes. This item comes in many varieties is destroyed when activated.
name: Weather Rocks

description: Pokémon class-exclusive. An item that negates the first 3 points of damage taken during a combat encounter. This item breaks apart one week after it is first used.
name: Egg Shell

description: An item that increases Cute by 2.
name: Cute Comforter

description: Pokémon class-exclusive. An item that increases a stat of your choice by 2 during a combat encounter. Only you can benefit from this item. The boosted stat cannot be changed once it has been chosen.
name: Your Item

description: An item that increases SPATK by 1 for 10 minutes when the holder uses a voice-oriented move. This effect cannot be stacked and can only be activated once a day.
name: Throat Spray

description: An item that increases Tough by 2.
name: Tough Tippet

description: An item that increases Beauty by 2.
name: Beautiful Bandana

description: An item that increases ATK by 2 but allows a Pokémon to use only 1 move for 3 minutes. If the move's frequency is consumed, the Pokémon may Struggle. This item does not stack with stat passives.
name: Choice Band

description: An item that allows the holder to use one of their moves with Priority. If another character is using a Priority move, compare their SPD. This item works once a day.
name: Quick Claw

description: An item that increases ATK by 2 on the holder's first turn in combat. This effect cannot be stacked with stat passives.
name: Muscle Band

description: An item that increases DEF and SPDEF by 1, if the holder can still evolve and does not have DEF- or SPDEF-raising passives.
name: Eviolite

description: An item that allows a move to be used if an opponent's effect prevents it from being used. When triggered, this new effect lasts for five minutes. This item is destroyed when consumed.
name: Mental Herb

description: An item that allows created, matching terrain to last for 5 minutes. This item comes in many varieties and is destroyed when activated.
name: Terrain Extender

description: An item that deals an additional 1d4 damage per turn to binding moves. This item is typically held.
name: Binding Band

description: An item that grants the holder immunity to Ground-type attacks. If they hiot by any other type of attack, the balloon is destroyed.
name: Air Balloon

description: An item that increases SPATK by 2 but allows a Pokémon to use only 1 move for 3 minutes. If the move's frequency is consumed, the Pokémon may Struggle. This item does not stack with stat passives.
name: Choice Specs

description: An item that increases SPD by 2 but allows a Pokémon to use only 1 move for 3 minutes. If the move's frequency is consumed, the Pokémon may Struggle. This item does not stack with stat passives.
name: Choice Scarf

description: An item that protects the holder from any damage they would take when making physical contact with an opponent.
name: Protective Pads

description: An item that causes the Pokémon that infatuated the holder to become Infatuated themselves.
name: Destiny Knot

description: An item that saves the holder from being knocked out. If the holder is about to reach 0 HP, roll 1d20. If they roll 17 or higher, their HP is instead set to 1 and the band is destroyed.
name: Focus Band

description: An item that increases ATK by 2 for 10 minutes when a Pokémon is hit by an Electric-type attack. This effect cannot be stacked, and the item is destroyed when triggered.
name: Cell Battery

description: An item that increases Clever by 2.
name: Clever Cravat

description: An item that saves the holder from being knocked out if they were at top health. If the holder is about to reach 0 HP and they were just at 100% HP, roll 1d20. If they roll 17 or higher, their HP is instead set to 1.
name: Focus Sash

description: An item that allows the holder to make a critical hit when the wearer naturally rolls 18-20 on an accuracy roll. This item works once a day.
name: Razor Claw

description: An item that restores 1d4 HP for the holder when they deal damage. This item works once per combat encounter.
name: Shell Bell

description: An item that increases Cool by 2.
name: Cool Kerchief

description: An item that increases SPATK by 2 for 10 minutes when a Pokémon is hit by an Water-type attack. This effect cannot be stacked, and the item is destroyed when triggered.
name: Absorb Bulb

description: An item that recovers 10 HP for a Pokémon every 6 seconds until it recovers either 100 HP in total or its max HP. This item is destroyed when consumed.
name: Leftovers

description: An item that recovers for a Poison-type Pokémon 1d6 HP every round for 2 minutes (20 rounds), or until the Pokémon is fully healed. It deals 1d6 damage for other Pokémon every round for 2 minutes, or until the Pokémon is knocked out. This item requires an action to be consumed and is destroyed after.
name: Black Sludge

description: A cellular device that features remote access to the Pokémon Computer Terminal network. The Box Link can access the PCT network anytime and anywhere, provided that it is charged and there is a clear and strong signal. The Box Link allows registered trainers to both deposit and withdraw Pokémon, taking about one minute to conduct an exchange. Box Links can last an entire week with a 3-hour charge and is capable of solar or outlet charging. The Box Link can also facilitate trades made through the Global Trade System (but not local ones). Injured Pokémon will not be healed when a transfer is made. 20 lbs. 1.5x1x0.5 ft.
name: Box Link

description: A Tera Orb is a conduit that passively gathers crystallized energy. With it, trainers can trigger the Terastalization phenomenon once a day (as a free action). Terastalization can only be induced on a Loyalty 2 or higher Pokémon. Terastalization lasts for 10 minutes, improves the Pokémon's defensive stats, and changes its typing into its Tera Type. If the Terastalized Pokémon is recalled or knocked out before 10 minutes runs, it reverts back to its standard form.
name: Tera Orb

description: A Dynamax band is the housing for a Wishing Star. With it, trainers can trigger the Dynamax or Gigantamax phenomena once a day (as a free action). Dynamax and Gigantamax can only be induced on a Loyalty 2 or higher Pokémon. Dynamax and Gigantamax last for 1 minute, increases the Pokémon's size immensely, multiplies their maximum HP by 5, sets its HP to full, and allows them to use the pool of Max moves only. Additionally, Gigantamax Pokémon take on a new form and gain access to a unique Max move. When the Pokémon changes back to normal, its current HP will be at max if it surpassed that value while Dynamaxed.
name: Dynamax Band

description: Typically designed as an accessory, a Mega Ring is the housing of a Keystone. With it, trainers can trigger the Mega Evolution phenomenon once a day (as a free action). Mega Evolution can only be induced on a Loyalty 2 or higher Pokémon holding its matching Mega Stone. Mega Evolution lasts for 10 minutes, generally improving the stats of the Pokémon and sometimes changing its typing and passives. If the Mega Evolved Pokémon is recalled or knocked it before the ten minutes runs out, it reverts back to its standard form.
name: Mega Ring

description: A Z-Ring is the housing for a Spakling Stone and a Z-Crystal. With it, trainers can empower their Pokémon and utilize Z-Moves once a day (as an action). A Z-Move can only be used with a Loyalty 2 or higher Pokémon and a compatible Z-Crystal. The Z-Move used, and the Pokémon that can use it, differs from crystal to crystal.
name: Z Ring

description: Snag Machines are illegal devices used to poach Pokémon. They are constructed to resemble gauntlets. Any Poké ball thrown using an active snag machine will be able to capture an owned Pokémon. | The lower the Pokémon's loyalty is to their trainer, the easier it will be for a snag machine to steal them. They are ineffective against Loyalty 3 Pokémon, however.
name: Snag Machine

description: Ranger Stylers are electronic Pokémon restraint systems that non-violently reign in wilds. They are used exclusively by rangers, though other members of law enforcement may be licensed and trained to use them. It is illegal for a civilian to have access to a stlyer. Stylers can last for an entire week on a 2-hour charge. Charging stations are accessible at ranger stations, law enforcement departments, or Pokémon centers. | As an action, a non-Ranger character may use the styler in attempt to restrain a wild Pokémon. Like with Poké balls, they will make a capture roll. If successful, the wild becomes Helpful. It will remain under the character's guidance until it is hit by an attack, distracted, or has helped you in a significant manner once (such as making an attack). The character can utilize only the skills or moves that they have deduced or observed. Attempting to take the wild out its territory will lead it to run away. A character may have only one Helpful Pokémon at a time.
name: Ranger Styler

description: A cellular device that features remote access to the Pokémon Computer Terminal network. The Box Link can access the PCT network anytime and anywhere, provided that it is charged and there is a clear and strong signal. The Box Link allows registered trainers to both deposit and withdraw Pokémon, taking about one minute to conduct an exchange. Box Links can last an entire week with a 3-hour charge and is capable of solar or outlet charging. The Box Link can also facilitate trades made through the Global Trade System (but not local ones). Injured Pokémon will not be healed when a transfer is made. 20 lbs. 1.5x1x0.5 ft.
name: Pokemon Box Link

description:
Short for Pokémon Gear, a mobile phone developed in the Johto region. It can access the Pokédex database and world map, store contact information, supports conference calls, features a GPS and other applications. This device can either be handheld or worn like a wristwatch.

A smart device that takes the worm of a wrist watch. These devices are manufactured by many companies, usually unique to each region. Each one can wirelessly connect to a smartphone or Pokédex, so that users can access their applications with extra convenience. On their own, these devices hold a record of trainer IDs, make long-distance video calls, offers a GPS (that works best when near a working cell tower), and various other applications.
name: Pokegear

description: Each of these smart devices are worn on your wrist and are wirelessly connected to your smart phone and/or Pokédex that enhances the convenience of many different applications. They can be used to keep track of other trainers’ trainer IDs that you’ve battled, provide face-to-face long calls, GPS location (assuming you’re close enough to a working cell tower), and various other smart device applications conveniently on your wrist on a small computer screen.
name: Poketch

description:
A Pokédex is an electronic device that collects and contains information on Pokémon. About the size of a smartphone, the Pokédex has an internal database that records and analyzes data relating to individual Pokémon and entire species. Similar to smartphones with the Pokédex app, this device features an advanced camera with an identification system that can bring up information about a targeted Pokémon, provided that the device is pointing at oone for at least three seconds. With a language UI, this information can be read aloud or pulled up through voice command.

The Pokédex patent is shared worldwide, with hundreds of varieties and new models coming out every year with better and more features. While some models have many similarities with smart devices, each Pokédex unit contributes to a worldwide knowledge base on each species of Pokémon. A Pokédex owner may contribute their observations and findings to the database for peer review.

Some manufacturers have designed their models to house a Rotom, granting the device a sentient controller that can further aid trainers. These Rotom are bred specifically to inhabit a Pokédex and do not count as one of the trainer's owned Pokémon. They are considered wild Pokémon who have bounded to a trainer's item. These models cannot be dematerialized by a Poké ball, so the Rotom is safe from capture.
name: Pokedex

description: Each of these smart devices are worn on your wrist and are wirelessly connected to your smart phone and/or Pokédex that enhances the convenience of many different applications. They can be used to keep track of other trainers’ trainer IDs that you’ve battled, provide face-to-face long calls, GPS location (assuming you’re close enough to a working cell tower), and various other smart device applications conveniently on your wrist on a small computer screen.
name: Vs Seeker

description: Each of these smart devices are worn on your wrist and are wirelessly connected to your smart phone and/or Pokédex that enhances the convenience of many different applications. name: Burn Heal

description: Spray-type medicine that cures all afflictions, except for the Cursed effect. It works on both humans and Pokémon.

description: Spray-type medicine that cures the Frozen affliction. It works on both humans and Pokémon.

description: Medicine that fully restores a Pokémon's strength. They can use all of their 1/day and 3/day frequency moves as if they have taken an extended rest.

description: Spray-type medicine that cures the Asleep affliction. It works on both humans and Pokémon.

description: Medicine that partially restores a Pokémon's strength. They can use one of their 1/day or 3/day frequency moves as if they have taken an extended rest.

description: Spray-type medicine that heals 10 HP for a Pokémon.
name: Potion

description:
One Portion of standard multi-diet Pokémon food pellets. Bland but edible.

When using the optional maintenance rule, daily units = ceil(½×size rank + ½×weight rank) from PTA biology classes (Tiny + Featherweight = 1 unit per day).
name: Pokémon Food

description: A sealed bag holding 50 portions of standard Pokémon Food pellets (same as single packs). Price matches 50× single portion. Track portions at the table as you use them.
name: Pokémon Food Bag

description: A meal in a bag containing three meals worth of food, ready to eat hot or cold. Contains three smaller bags each containing the contents of a Trainer Ration - One Meal. Contains a Breakfast, Lunch, and Dinner menu, plus six water purification tablets.
name: Trainer Ration - 24 Hour

description: Holds up to 12 Trainer Ration units. The container is waterproof and heat tolerant.
name: Trainer Ration - Container

description: A concoction that wild Pokémon find repulsive, keeping them away for one hour. Some determined Pokémon may ignore the smell to protect something valuable to them.
name: Repel

description: Researcher-exclusive. A homemade concoction that wild Pokémon find repulsive, keeping them away for half an hour. Some determined Pokémon may ignore the smell to protect something valuable to them.
name: Homemade Repel

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -8 to your capture roll if the Pokémon acts before your Pokémon.
name: Fast Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -15 to your capture roll if the Pokémon is Grass- or Ground-type.
name: Earth Ball

description: A standard-issue ball that grants +5 to your capture roll.
name: Basic Ball

description: A competition-grade ball that grants -20 to your capture roll against domesticated wild Pokémon.
name: Park Ball

description: A standard-issue ball that grants +0 to your capture roll.
name: Great Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -12 to your capture roll if you are in a Cave environment.
name: Cave Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -12 to your capture roll if you are in a Marsh environment.
name: Marsh Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -10 to your capture roll if the Pokémon's gender is opposite to your Pokémon.
name: Love Ball

description: A specialized ball that grants -10 to your capture roll if the Pokémon already had a ball thrown at it this encounter.
name: Repeat Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -12 to your capture roll if you are in a Forest environment.
name: Forest Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -15 to your capture roll if the Pokémon is Electric- or Fire-type.
name: Heat Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -12 to your capture roll if you are in a Desert environment.
name: Desert Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -10 to your capture roll if the Pokémon is unconscious. The Save Ball will not negatively affect the captured Pokémon's loyalty.
name: Save Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -10 to your capture roll if you lured the wild Pokémon into combat.
name: Lure Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -15 to your capture roll if the Pokémon is Dragon- or Psychic-type.
name: Mystic Ball

description: A specialized ball that grants +0 to your capture roll. Captured Pokémon restore 20 HP.
name: Heal Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -15 to your capture roll if the Pokémon is Flying- or Ice-type.
name: Air Ball

description: A rarely-manufactured ball that grants -10 to your capture roll if the Pokémon is afflicted.
name: Dream Ball

description: A specialized ball that grants -10 to your capture roll if the Pokémon can evolve, but is not evolved.
name: Nest Ball

description: A rarely-manufactured ball that grants -5 to your capture roll. Captured Pokémon gain an additional point of Loyalty.
name: Cherish Ball

description: A specialized ball that grants -15 to your capture roll if the Pokémon is Bug- or Water-type.
name: Net Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -10 to your capture roll if the Pokémon is an evolution stage lower than your Pokémon.
name: Level Ball

description: A rarely-manufactured ball that grants -100 to your capture roll. The Master Ball can and will often fail when thrown at legendary Pokémon.
name: Master Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -12 to your capture roll if you are in a Mountain environment.
name: Mountain Ball

description: A specialized ball that grants -12 to your capture roll if the Pokémon is aquatic.
name: Dive Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -15 to your capture roll if the Pokémon is Rock- or Steel-type.
name: Solid Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -12 to your capture roll if you are in an Arctic environment.
name: Arctic Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -12 to your capture roll if you are in a Freshwater environment.
name: Freshwater Ball

description: A competition-grade ball that grants -20 to your capture roll against domesticated wild Pokémon.
name: Safari Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -12 to your capture roll if you are in a Taiga environment.
name: Taiga Ball

description: A specialized ball that grants -7 to your capture roll if you make the throw at night or without no visible sunlight.
name: Dusk Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -12 to your capture roll if you are in a Grassland environment.
name: Grassland Ball

description: A specialized ball that grants -10 to your capture roll if the encounter has lasted at least 1 minute; -25 from your capture roll if the encounter has lasted at least 2 minutes.
name: Timer Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -12 to your capture roll if you are in an Urban environment.
name: Urban Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -12 to your capture roll if you are in a Tundra environment.
name: Tundra Ball

description: A promotional ball that grants -5 to your capture roll. Captured Pokémon gain an additional point of Loyalty.
name: Premier Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -15 to your capture roll if the Pokémon is Poison- or Fighting-type.
name: Mold Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -12 to your capture roll if you are in a Rainforest environment.
name: Rainforest Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -12 to your capture roll if you are in a Beach environment.
name: Beach Ball

description: A specialized ball that grants -5 to your capture roll. Captured Pokémon gain an additional point of Loyalty.
name: Luxury Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -15 to your capture roll if the Pokémon is Heavy or Superweight.
name: Heavy Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants +0 to your capture roll. Captured Pokémon gain an additional point of Loyalty.
name: Friend Ball

description: A competition-grade ball that grants -20 to your capture roll against domesticated wild Pokémon.
name: Sport Ball

description: A standard-issue ball that grants -5 to your capture roll.
name: Ultra Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -15 to your capture roll if the Pokémon is Dark- or Ghost-type.
name: Haunt Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -15 to your capture roll if the Pokémon is Normal- or Fairy-type.
name: Fine Ball

description: A specially-crafted ball that grants -10 to your capture roll if the Pokémon can evolve with an evolution stone.
name: Moon Ball

description: A specialized ball that grants -20 to your capture roll if you make the throw as your first action in combat.
name: Quick Ball

description: A training disc that teaches 3/day frequency moves. This item can be used up to three times.
name: Tm 3 Day

description: A training disc that teaches 1/day frequency moves. This item can be used up to three times.
name: Tm 1 Day

description: A training disc that teaches at-will frequency moves. This item can be used up to three times.
name: Tm At Will

description: Special Operative-exclusive (Ranger). A special device that launches stun powder grenades, settling dangerous situations quickly. Special Operative rangers are sanctioned to carry 3 charges for this launcher. In order to restock their charges, rangers must report to a ranger station. | This item grants access to the move Stun Gas.
name: Tactical Launcher Stun

description: Designer-exclusive (Coordinator). A custom item that adds 2 to a skill check while talking to someone else. On a successful roll, this item cannot be used this way again until it has been repaired (with the Practical Designer feature). This effect does not stack.
name: Trainer Accessory

description: Special Operative-exclusive (Ranger). A special component that allows a capture styler to restrain owned Pokémon. This component was designed to combat poachers and criminals who abuse Pokémon. | 3/day. As an action, the wielder may use the move Wrap on a Pokémon. Pokémon bound by this function cannot move or use attacks, unless they break free (with a disadvantaged, contested Athletics check).
name: Coiled Styler

description: Special Operative-exclusive (Ranger). A special device that launches webs to capture fleeing criminals. Highly-decorated Special Operative rangers are sanctioned to carry 3 charges for this launcher. In order to restock their charges, rangers must report to a ranger station. | This item grants access to the move Spider Webbing.
name: Tactical Launcher Web

description: Special Operative-exclusive (Ranger). A special component that allows a capture styler to produce an energy shield. This component was designed to protect its wielder. | Accuracy rolls made when attacking the wielder or an adjacent ally have their DC increased by 1. If the opponent fails their accuracy roll, the shield shatters. The component must charge for 1 minute before it can be used again. This component is not active when the coiled attachment is being used.
name: Shield Styler

description: An electronic, reuseable container that keeps Pokémon eggs warm and safe. This item lasts can protect an egg for a month on a 4-hour charge. It is sturdy enough to be dropped from 100 ft without harming the egg. It is also watertight and insulating. Although heavy, an incubator is much more convenient than carrying an egg on its own. Carrying 2 can mean for tight luggage. 15 lbs. 16x8x8 in. 4 ft. strap.
name: Egg Incubator

description: A portable 3-speed bike. It features telescoping handles and an adjustable seat for any height. 25 lbs. 26 in (wheels). 48x40 in (unfolded), 30x30x6 (unfolded).
name: Folding Bike

description: Designer-exclusive (Coordinator). A custom item that grants advantage on a skill check. On a failed roll, you fumble and take 1d10 damage. This item cannot be used again until it has been repaired (with the Lucky Charm feature). This effect does not stack.
name: Lucky Charm

description: Special Operative-exclusive (Ranger). A special device that launches knock-out gas. Highly-decorated Special Operative rangers are sanctioned to carry 3 charges for this launcher. In order to restock their charges, rangers must report to a ranger station. | This item grants access to the move Sleep Gas.
name: Tactical Launcher Sleep

description: A special mask that allows the wearer to breathe underwater for up to 30 minutes. After 15 minutes of use, it will need to be cleaned, dried, and allowed to charge for another hour.
name: Rebreather

description: Special Operative-exclusive (Ranger). A special device that launches a smoke screen, allowing for quick getaways, escapes, or cover during raids. Special Operative rangers are sanctioned to carry 3 charges for this launcher. In order to restock their charges, rangers must report to a ranger station. | This item grants access to the move Smoke Screen.
name: Tactical Launcher Smoke

description: Special Operative-exclusive (Ranger). A pair of goggles equipped with specialized scanners designed to assess disastrous situations. These goggles can see through fog, smoke, sandstorms, and any other moving non-permanent visual obstruction. | 5/day. As a free action, the wielder may reveal the target Pokémon's current HP, relative to its total HP stat.
name: Scanner Goggles

description: Pokémon class-exclusive. An item that increases a stat of your choice by 2 during a combat encounter. Only you can benefit from this item. The boosted stat cannot be changed once it has been chosen.
name: Trainer Equipment

description: Designer-exclusive (Coordinator). A custom item that adds 1 to a skill check when using the item for its intended purpose. This item loses this effect for two weeks or until it is repaired (with the Tactical Design feature). This effect does not stack.
name: Clothing Equipment

description: Apparel carefully designed to protect humans in sandstorms, desert winds, and hot conditions. | Humans will not take damage in the Sandstorm weather effect.
name: Desert Gear

description: A special piece of clothing that grants +2 to a Bluff / Deception skill check.
name: Domino Mask

description: A special piece of clothing that grants +2 to an Athletics skill check.
name: Bracing Sleeves

description: Special Operative-exclusive (Ranger). A set of protective gear that reduces the damage of Attack moves by 5.
name: Riot Gear

description: A special piece of clothing that grants +3 Defense during accuracy checks made against you and you move 20ft less per turn. 50 lbs.
name: Full Plate Armor

description: A special piece of clothing that grants +2 to a Constitution skill check.
name: Padded Jacket

description: A special piece of clothing that grants +2 to an Acrobatics skill check.
name: Ninja Tabi

description: A kit equipped with everything a Poké ball smith needs. One kit includes an electric soldering table (2x2 ft), a soldering iron, a desoldering pump, 8 assorted soldering iron tips, a tin wire tube, a pair of tweezers, a wire stripper cutter, 80 ft of electrical wire, a micro screwdriver, 400 micro-screws, a micro glass, 200 spare mirrors, 200 micro rods, and 200 micro steel sheets. The table folds into itself while storing all of the supplies, complete with a handle. 15 lbs. 24x12x4 in.
name: Pokeball Smith Kit

description: A kit equipped for the basics for Pokémon grooming, ideal for typical trainers. One kit includes an electronic trimmer, 8 trimmer length attachments, 5 assorted combs, 5 assorted brushes, 3 assorted scissors/shears (3), a pair of clippers, a nail file, a bar of soap (8 oz.), a bar of shampoo (8 oz.), and a bag. The trimmer last one week on an hour-long charge. 4 lbs. 12x8x8 in. 2ft strap.
name: Grooming Kit

description: A kit equipped with everything needed to enjoy a meal on the go. One kit includes a plate, a bowl, a fork, a spoon, a knife, a cup, and a tray. 1.5 lbs. 10x10x3 in.
name: Mess Kit

description: A kit equipped with everything a digger needs. One kit includes an extendable shovel, an extendable pickaxe, a hammer, a trowel, a compass, a 25 ft measuring tape, 10 6" stakes (10), 600 ft of string, and a tool roll bag with room for more equipment. 15 lbs. 16x6x4 in. 4ft strap.
name: Dig Kit

description: A micro-environment designed to rapidly cultivate berries. A shrub planted from a single berry can eventually sprout 2–3 new berries. 3 lbs. 2x1x1 ft. 4 ft strap. | When this device is used, roll a Nature check. Then once a day for the next 5 days, water the plant and roll another Nature check. If you roll below 6 even once, then the plant will yield 1 berry. Rolling over 20 will yield 3 berries. Otherwise, it will bear 2. Failing to water the shrub and/or harvesting the berries will kill the plant.
name: Berry Planter

description: A bag equipped with everything a travelling photographer needs, minus the camera. One bag includes 3 lenses, 2 extra bulbs, gaffer tape, microfiber cloth, a micro screwdriver, a micro flashlight, an spare battery, a 20 GB memory card, and ear plugs. 18x10x8 in. 3 lbs.
name: Camera Bag

description: A kit designed for extended travel. One kit includes a collapsible pop-up tent (two-person, 7x4 ft), a tarp (8x10 ft), a sleeping bag, a pocketknife, a solar flashlight, a compass, 20 waterproof matches, water bottle, and a compression sack. 15 lbs. 40x16x16 in.
name: Travelers Pack

description: There’s no real reason to have this, let alone sell it. This may not be easily found and available in stores. Includes, solar charger, universal power adapter, universal adapter dongle, memory sticks (4 80 GB), micro file (2), lock picks (8), multi-head screwdriver, hammer, crowbar, small narrow saw, small narrow scissors, telescoping mirror, thin wire (20 ft), water bottle, and a backpack or side pack to fit everything with some extra room. 10 lbs. 30x16x16 in.
name: Miscreant’s Pack

description: A first aid kid designed for medical professionals and overprotective parents. One kit includes pain relievers (20 pills), antihistamine (20 pills), cold/flu relievers (20pills), electrolyte tablets (20 pills), antiseptic solution (8 oz.), antiseptic gel (8 oz.), 30 ft of medical adhesive tape, 2 slings, 2 rolls of crepe bandages (3 ft each), 4 rolls of cloth bandages (3 ft each), 20 gauze swabs, 20 stitch strips, 10 sets of ear plugs, 20 safety pins, thermometer, small scissors, small scalpel, tweezers, and a box of 40 assorted adhesive bandages, and a carrying case. 10 lbs. 2x1.5x.025 ft. | This kit can be used to stabilize humans, but is less useful with Pokémon unless the owner is proficient with medicine. As an action, a character may use this kit and attempt a Medicine check (difficulty 15) to stabilize an unconscious human, bringing them to 1 HP. Out of combat, the character may take 5 minutes to stabilize an unconscious human. Out of combat, with a difficulty 15 Medicine check, the character may cure a target of any affliction (once an hour). As this kit is used, it will need to be restocked.
name: Medical Field Kit

description: A set of tools for basic repairs and maintenance. One kit comes in a tackle box and includes a hammer, a screwdriver with an adaptable head, a wrench, a handsaw, a steel ruler, a level, 40 assorted nails, 40 assorted screws, 40 assorted nuts and bolts. 8 lbs. 14x6x6 in.
name: Basic Tool Kit

description: A kit equipped with everything an engineer needs. One kit includes an electric soldering table (2x2 ft), a soldering iron, a desoldering pump, 8 assorted soldering iron tips, 2 soldering assist tools, tin wire tube, tweezers, a wire stripper cutter, 80 ft of electric wire, 2 longnose pliers, a precision screwdriver, a magnetic/gripping retrieving tool, a telescoping mirror, 100 various spare screws. The table folds into itself while storing all of the supplies, complete with a handle. 15 lbs. 24x12x4 in.
name: Engineers Tool Kit

description: A kit equipped with everything a chef on-the-go needs. One kit includes a portable 2-burner electric stove, a cast iron pan, a cast iron pot with lid, a water purifier, a cutting board, a knife, measuring cups and spoons, a spatula, a cooking spoon, and a sturdy bag. The electric stove can be used for 2 weeks after a 4-hour charge. 35 lbs. 2x2x1 ft. 4 ft strap.
name: Cooking Kit

description: A kit equipped for the basics for Pokémon fishing, ideal for typical trainers. One kit includes a telescoping fishing rod, 2 spare reel lines, a set of 100 assorted hooks, 10 assorted bobbers, 10 sinkers, a set of 10 assorted lures, a pair of needlenose pliers, a small knife, and a tackle box. 8 lbs. 14x6x6 in.
name: Fishing Kit

description: A kit equipped with everything a drawer or a doodler needs. One kit includes a drawing pad (200 pages), a journal (200 pages), 10 blue pencils, 2 mechanical pencils, a kneaded eraser, a white vinyl eraser, a blender, pen and ink, 10 assorted pen brushes, and a bag with to carry it all with room to spare. 4 lbs. 20x18x3 in. 3ft strap.
name: Drawing Kit

description: A video game entertainment system that comes with a case and charging cable. Compatible games are available from 20 to 80. Advanced Pokémon are known to play some of the simpler games. 1 lb. 8x4x1 in.
name: Nintendo Switch

description: Stores Pokéballs efficiently. Holds up to 12 balls.
name: Pokeball Container
